description.value: Masterless, distributed wide-column NoSQL database designed for linear scalability and high availability across multiple data centers

first-release.comment: Initial release in 2008 (originally developed at Facebook, open-sourced via Apache).

latest-version.comment: Latest GA version as of 2026-06-09; 4.1.11 is the prior stable line.

database-type.comment: Wide-column store based on ideas from BigTable and Dynamo; adds vector support in 5.0.

query-language.comment: Uses CQL (Cassandra Query Language), an SQL-like language; no dedicated CQL tag in the schema, so mapped to the closest 'sql' tag.

acid-compliant.comment: AP system with tunable, eventual consistency by default; only lightweight (Paxos) transactions provide limited atomicity. Not full ACID by default per Assessment Guidelines.

schema-model.comment: CQL tables require column definitions; schema is enforced at the table level.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Native masterless distribution; linear scalability is a core design goal.

replication.comment: Masterless architecture; every node accepts writes (peer-to-peer), equivalent to multi-primary replication.

clustering.comment: Built-in peer-to-peer clustering across nodes and data centers without third-party tools.

cloud-offering.comment: Managed Cassandra-as-a-service offered via DataStax Astra DB (now IBM) and AWS Keyspaces; not run by the Apache Foundation itself.

full-text-search.comment: Storage-Attached Indexing (SAI) in 5.0 adds full-text/secondary search capabilities.

json-support.comment: CQL supports INSERT JSON and SELECT JSON for reading/writing rows as JSON; not a native document model.

geospatial.comment: No native geospatial data types or queries in core CQL.

time-series.comment: Commonly used for time-series workloads via wide rows and clustering keys, but offers no specialized/dedicated time-series engine or functions.

stored-procedures.comment: Server-side user-defined functions (UDFs) and aggregates (UDAs) are supported; no traditional stored procedures.

triggers.comment: CQL supports CREATE TRIGGER with custom Java trigger implementations.

views.comment: Materialized views are supported (historically flagged experimental).

official-gui.comment: No official graphical administration tool shipped by the Apache project; third-party tools exist.

cli-tool.comment: Ships with cqlsh (CQL shell) and nodetool for administration.

docker-image.comment: Docker Official Image maintained by the Docker Community.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Could not fetch the Stack Overflow 'cassandra' tag question count from a reliable source during this pass (stackoverflow.com not fetchable).

db-engines-rank.comment: Ranked #10 overall and #1 among wide-column stores as of 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: CQL syntax is SQL-like, but distributed data modeling (partition/clustering keys, denormalization, tunable consistency) adds significant conceptual complexity; rated relatively steep.
